About: It is about an intentional, vigorous promulgation of ideas.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Communist Party of the Soviet Union.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the party apparatus.
ConsistsOf: It consists of popular media, such as literature, plays, pamphlets, films, and other art forms.
CreatedIn: It was created in the Soviet Union.
CreatedTo: It was created to promulgate ideas.
HasA: It has an explicitly political message in favor of communism.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by an intentional, vigorous promulgation of ideas.
IsA: It is a portmanteau of agitation and propaganda.
It is a shortened name for the Department for Agitation and Propaganda.
It is a highly politicized theatre.
It is any kind of highly politicized art.
KnownFor: It is known for its role in the Soviet Union.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Russian Civil War.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Soviet Union.
It occurred in the United States.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the Soviet Union.
PartOf: It is part of the central and regional committees of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union.
UsedFor: It is used for dissemination of information and knowledge.
It is used for promoting the ideology and policies of the Communist Party.
It is used for persuading the general public to support and join the party.
It is used for sharing the party's ideals.
It is used for disseminating information and knowledge.
It is used for spreading new methods of agriculture.
It is used for performing simple plays.
It is used for broadcasting propaganda.
It is used for reproducing posters.
It is used for creating more instructors.
It is used for explaining Bolshevik ideology.
UsedIn: It is used in literature, plays, pamphlets, and films.
It is used in the press.
It is used in oral-agitation networks.
It is used in agitational trains and ships.
It is used in literacy campaigns.
UsedTo: It is used to explain the ideology and policies of the Communist Party.
It is used to attempt to persuade the general public to support and join the party.
It is used to share the party's ideals.
It is used to disseminate information and knowledge to the people.
It is used to spread new methods of agriculture.
It is used to tour the country.
It is used to perform simple plays.
It is used to broadcast propaganda.
It is used to reproduce posters.
It is used to expand the reach of the oral-agitation networks.
